provenance.source_corpus: Efficacy of releasing captive reared broodstock into an imperilled wild Atlantic salmon population as a recovery strategy

nodes.name: releasing captive reared adult Atlantic salmon

source_spans.text: The strategy of releasing captive reared adult Atlantic salmonSalmo salar into the Magaguadavic River, New Brunswick, Canada, to spawn, was not an effective tool for rebuilding a seriously depressed wild population.

nodes.name: rebuilding a seriously depressed wild population

source_spans.text: The strategy of releasing captive reared adult Atlantic salmonSalmo salar into the Magaguadavic River, New Brunswick, Canada, to spawn, was not an effective tool for rebuilding a seriously depressed wild population.
The following year few to no Atlantic salmon fry were found, and most appeared not to be offspring of released adults.

Microsatellite genetic analysis was used to determine that 9 % (three of 32 fish) of the Atlantic salmon fry collected in Linton Stream were compatible with single (one maternal, two paternal) sexually mature Floy tagged captive reared parents released the previous autumn.
